_doc: Canary topics for /deep-research scheduled validation. Each canary is a stable-answer topic with a known τ and source-count baseline; drift from these baselines on a quick-depth run is an early-warning signal that something in the deep-research pipeline (fetcher routing, triangulator scoring, verifier re-fetch, schema versioning) has regressed. Authored: BG-23 (F4.3). Authorship: Enchanter Labs.
_rotation_strategy: round-robin via index_file (state/scheduled-validation-index.txt). Each run picks index, runs that canary, increments index modulo len(canaries). Random-pick was rejected because run-to-run comparability requires we hit each canary on a known cadence — a 5-canary list with random pick would mean some canaries get re-run before others get a first run, which hurts τ-drift detection.
_quick_depth_only: All canaries run at --depth quick. Quick depth = ~6 fetchers, 1 round, 1 triangulator pass, 1 verifier pass. Cost envelope ~$1-3 per run. Full depth (~$5-15) is reserved for principal-initiated work, not validation.
_drift_definition: A run is drift-flagged if any of: verdict != PARTIAL_QUICK; τ drifts by > 0.15 from baseline_tau (absolute); sources_count drops below baseline_sources_count_min; wall_clock_ms below 60_000 (quick-depth floor — under one minute means a phase short-circuited); claims_count below baseline_claims_min; schema parse failure on claims.json.
_operator_action_on_drift: Read state/roadmaps/scheduled-validation-drift-YYYY-MM-DD.md, run the canary again manually in foreground at --depth quick, diff the new claims.json against the saved baseline brief, and if the regression reproduces, open a P1 against the offending phase.

canaries.topic: What is the maximum context window of Claude Sonnet 4.5 as of the model's documented spec?
canaries.rationale: Vendor-documented model spec. Answer is a single integer (1_000_000 tokens for the 1M-context variant; 200_000 for the standard variant). Triangulator should easily reach high τ; if it doesn't, fetcher routing or source-discipline weighting has regressed.
canaries.stable_until: next model rev — re-baseline when Anthropic changes context-window numbers

canaries.topic: What is the current REST endpoint path for OpenAI's chat completions API and what HTTP method does it require?
canaries.rationale: POST /v1/chat/completions. Stable for years. Multiple independent sources (OpenAI docs, SDK source, community refs). High-confidence fetch target — used as a ground-truth control.
canaries.stable_until: OpenAI restructures the v1 routes (unlikely in 90d)

canaries.topic: What is MMLU-Pro and how many multiple-choice questions does it contain in the standard release?
canaries.rationale: MMLU-Pro is a well-documented benchmark with a known question count (~12_032). Tests benchmark-shape SQ decomposition. Should have high inter-source agreement.

canaries.topic: What are the canonical ways to create a NumPy array from a Python list, and what dtype inference rule applies?
canaries.rationale: Long-stable library API (numpy.array, numpy.asarray, np.fromiter). Tests library-shape SQ decomposition. Many independent tutorial sources — high redundancy is a feature for this canary, since it tests source-independence dedup.

canaries.topic: What does HTTP status code 429 mean and what header is conventionally used to communicate retry timing?
canaries.rationale: RFC 6585 + the Retry-After header. Standards-track answer with multiple independent specifications. Tests spec-shape SQ decomposition and RFC-source recognition.
canaries.stable_until: IETF retires RFC 6585 (effectively never on this cadence)
